book_summary: Judges has chapters 1-10 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book now covers incomplete conquest, forced labor, Bochim, the judges cycle, Othniel, Ehud, Shamgar, Deborah, Barak, Jael, the Song of Deborah, Gideon's call under Midianite economic devastation, YHWH-shalom, altar destruction, fleece signs, the reduced army, Midian's panic, Oreb and Zeeb, Gideon's de-escalation with Ephraim, revenge against Succoth and Penuel, execution of Zebah and Zalmunna, kingship refusal paired with quasi-royal accumulation, the ephod snare, Abimelech's fratricidal kingship, Jotham's bramble fable, Shechem's destruction, Thebez and the millstone woman, Tola, Jair, renewed apostasy, Ammonite oppression, and YHWH's grief over Israel's misery. Safeguards reject conquest triumphalism, torture, mutilation, forced labor, victim-blaming, religious persecution, ethnic-purity readings, body shaming, disability shame, assassination glorification, noncombatant shaming, sexual-violence normalization, religious vandalism, mob punishment, coercive sign-seeking, reckless under-resourcing, psychological warfare glorification, trophy violence, revenge torture, child execution training, leader cults, violent monarchy, maternal-status shaming, ableist evil-spirit language, misogynistic honor codes, and claims that Divine rebuke means indifference to suffering.

chapters.notes: Midianite economic devastation, prophet rebuke, Gideon's hidden wheat and protest, angelic call, YHWH-shalom, Baal/Asherah altar destruction by night, Joash's de-escalation, Spirit clothing Gideon, tribal summons, and fleece signs.

Jerubbaal/Gideon, fearful troop release, opaque water-lapping test, Purah and dream reassurance, barley loaf dream, jars/torches/trumpets night panic, Moabite self-slaughter, pursuit, Oreb/Zeeb execution, and trophy-head scene.

Ephraim conflict de-escalation, weary pursuit, Succoth/Penuel refusal and Gideon's revenge, Zebah and Zalmunna execution, Jether's fear, kingship refusal, gold ephod snare, seventy sons, Abimelech, and post-Gideon relapse.

Abimelech's Shechem kinship appeal, Baal-berith silver, hired reckless men, slaughter of seventy brothers, Jotham's tree fable, three-year rule, evil spirit, Gaal revolt, Shechem destruction and salting, tower burning, Thebez millstone woman, misogynistic death anxiety, and bloodguilt return.

Tola and Jair minor-judge notices, Jair's thirty sons/donkeys/towns, multi-deity apostasy list, Philistine and Ammonite oppression, YHWH's refusal and grief over Israel's misery, foreign-god removal, Mizpah/Gilead mobilization, and leadership bargain setting up Jephthah.

translation_notes: Judges opens by undoing any simple total-conquest reading from Joshua; the land is mixed, contested, partially controlled, and ethically compromised.
Forced-labor notices preserve survival under domination, not peaceful integration or justice.
The judges cycle must not become victim-blaming; the theological anchor is YHWH's pity and grief in response to the groaning and misery of oppressed people.
Achsah's repeated water request shows that land without life-sustaining resources is not meaningful inheritance.

Baal/Asherah polemic, altar destruction, and intermarriage concerns belong to Israel's covenant rhetoric and may not justify modern religious persecution, racism, xenophobia, anti-intermarriage ideology, or religious vandalism.
Ehud and Jael narratives preserve resistance violence under oppression but cannot become general permission for assassination, domestic violence, or political murder.
Deborah's authority as prophetess and judge is public, legal, and spiritual; it is not secondary to Barak.
The Song of Deborah is ancient war poetry; stars, Kishon, curse, and triumph images must not be forced into science claims or modern militarized spirituality.
Gideon's story repeatedly accommodates fear: hidden wheat, protest, night obedience, Purah, and fleece signs all prevent courage from being defined as fearlessness.
The reduced-army story prevents self-boasting but must not justify reckless under-resourcing, spiritualized scarcity, or forcing vulnerable people into danger.
Gideon's post-victory revenge against Succoth and Penuel shows deliverance curdling into coercion and retaliation.
Gideon's refusal of kingship is undermined by gold, many wives, seventy sons, a son named Abimelech, and a shrine-object snare.
Jotham's tree fable is a major anti-tyrant warning: fruitful vocation refuses bramble domination.
The evil spirit in Judges 9 must not be weaponized against mentally ill or neurodivergent people.
Abimelech's fear of being remembered as killed by a woman exposes misogynistic honor anxiety, not valid shame.
Judges 10's statement that YHWH's soul was shortened/grieved by Israel's misery blocks claims that rebuke means Divine indifference.
Mystical companion layers remain interpretive and never replace restored/source-audited text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Judges 1 relationship to Joshua conquest/allotment traditions, Jerusalem notices, Hebron/Debir/Achsah parallels, and forced-labor formulas require source and compositional audit.
Judges 2 Bochim/Gilgal movement, angel/messenger identity, Timnath-heres versus Timnath-serah, Baal/Ashtaroth terms, and cycle theology require textual and reception-history audit.

Judges 3 remaining-nations list, teaching-war language, Cushan-rishathaim, Ehud's right-hand phrase, Eglon satire, carved-stones/Gilgal wording, and Shamgar son of Anath require philological and historical audit.
Judges 4-5 prose/poetry differences, eshet Lappidoth, Deborah's judicial role, Jael's tent setting, archaic Hebrew, Meroz, and sexual-spoil imagery require careful philological and ethical audit.

Judges 6 Gideon/Jerubbaal names, Ophrah location, angel/YHWH shifts, YHWH-shalom, Baal/Asherah household altar, Joash's defense, Spirit clothing, and fleece signs require textual and reception-history audit.
Judges 7 troop-reduction logic, water-lapping selection, Purah, barley-loaf dream, Midianite panic, geography of pursuit, and Oreb/Zeeb locations require philological and military-history audit.
Judges 8 Succoth/Penuel punishment, Zebah/Zalmunna dialogue, Jether's age, ephod terminology, gold weight, Ishmaelite/Midianite overlap, and Abimelech naming require ethical and source audit.

Judges 9 Abimelech's Shechem kinship, Baal-berith/El-berith, Jotham's fable, evil-spirit wording, Gaal/Zebul revolt, salt-sowing, tower burning, Thebez millstone, and gendered honor death require textual and political-theology audit.
Judges 10 minor-judge notices, Jair/Havvoth-jair traditions, multi-deity list, Maon textual issue, YHWH's grief phrase, and Gilead leadership bargain require philological and compositional audit.
next_actions: Continue Judges in a larger batch with chapters 11-16: Jephthah's outcast origin, Ammonite negotiation, catastrophic vow and daughter, intertribal violence and Shibboleth, Ibzan/Elon/Abdon, Samson's birth, marriage, riddles, foxes, jawbone, Gaza, Delilah, blinding, and temple collapse—with maximum anti-child-sacrifice, anti-vow-abuse, anti-tribal-violence, anti-misogyny, anti-disability-shame, anti-suicide-misuse, and coercive-relationship safeguards.
